observation: baseline: naive O(n^3) triple-loop matrix multiplication
sparse DOK: row-indexed B, skip zeros in both A and B — 68.8x speedup
fully sparse A+B list rows, local var caching — 16% improvement
C extension via ctypes: CSR sparse matmul in C — 324x vs baseline, 4x vs exp3
dense-input C: moving all elements (incl zeros) through Python-C boundary slower than CSR-only marshaling
zero-copy buffer passing + optimized CSR build — 34% improvement over exp4
direct A-scan (no CSR for A) + CSR B only — 10% improvement, simpler code
